question: What is a smart contract in blockchain technology?
expectedPoints: Self-executing contracts with terms directly written into code
Automatically execute actions when conditions are met
Run on the blockchain, so they are decentralized and tamper-proof

question: Explain how Proof of Work (PoW) consensus mechanism operates in blockchain.
expectedPoints: Miners compete to solve complex mathematical puzzles
First miner to solve the puzzle gets to add the new block and is rewarded
Requires significant computational power and energy
Provides security through the difficulty of mining
The solved puzzle is easily verifiable by other nodes

question: Discuss the advantages and limitations of using blockchain technology in supply chain management.
expectedPoints: Advantage: Transparency - All transactions are recorded and visible to authorized participants
Advantage: Traceability - Every step of the supply chain can be tracked, reducing fraud
Advantage: Immutability - Records cannot be altered, ensuring data integrity
Advantage: Automation - Smart contracts can automate processes like payments and transfers
Limitation: Scalability - Blockchain networks can be slow and have limited transaction throughput
Limitation: Integration - Difficult to integrate with existing legacy systems
Limitation: Cost - Implementing and maintaining blockchain can be expensive
Limitation: Regulatory uncertainty - Lack of clear regulations in many regions
Limitation: Energy consumption - Especially for PoW blockchains, high energy use
